content:
# Cross Correlation

**Summary**: A mathematical operation that compares the similarity of two signals to identify common features and remove incoherent noise.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016]] used cross-correlation to generate virtual traces from raw seismic data to isolate head waves.

## Instruments and Software



## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Cross-correlation of pairs of traces from the same source resulted in 210 traces per receiver line, effectively filtering out non-coherent signals like ground roll and multiples. ([[2016-edigbue|Edigbue, 2016, p. 43]])
